<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This presents a challenge for the boy Samuel because he's picking up the divine voice,<|9.04|> <|9.04|> but the radio frequency is weak.<|13.30|> because in hearing these fractals of revelation,<|7.58|> <|8.14|> he needs the guidance and input of a wise mentor, a person born at a time when a word<|18.30|> <|18.30|> from the Lord was as commonly plentiful as ripe pomegranates.<|23.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> between October and March.<|2.66|> <|3.12|> Samuel, Samuel, the young boy hears under the cover of You called me.<|14.72|> <|15.96|> I did not call. Lie down again.<|20.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As this is taking place, Eli knows that Samuel is hearing God's voice, that the rarity of<|9.36|> <|9.36|> prophetic visions and voices is falling, and Eli remembers his own experience of hearing<|17.28|> <|17.28|> God's voice and uses it as an opportunity not to rush the moment.<|22.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> but help the budding prophet lean into the deep listening required in life with God.<|7.84|> <|9.84|> Martin Luther King Jr., raised in an intergenerational household,<|15.34|> <|15.34|> no doubt heard stories from the mouth of his maternal grandmother,<|20.38|> <|20.38|> Ginny Celeste Williams.<|23.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> who attended Spelman Seminary and married at age 26, old for that time.<|8.00|> <|8.00|> Lessons that guided his countercultural vision of a nation living out of its potential,<|16.00|> <|16.00|> not prejudicial impulses.<|20.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> A pastor's wife and a daughter of formerly enslaved people,<|5.00|> <|6.26|> Jenny Williams was a bridge from another century<|9.86|> <|9.86|> for Martin Luther King Jr.<|11.56|> <|13.68|> In partnership with her husband, the Reverend A.D. Williams,<|18.10|> <|18.10|> they led and nourished the people<|20.90|> <|20.90|> of Atlanta's Ebenezer Baptist Church<|23.64|> <|23.64|> for a quarter of a century.<|25.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> marrying social action and contemplation in the early 20th century.<|6.94|> <|8.10|> For Jenny and A.D., their black Christian faith guided them to <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.52|> It takes humility to listen to one another<|5.46|> <|5.46|> and learn from one another<|8.50|> <|8.50|> as we hear God call our own voices<|13.24|> <|13.24|> in the middle of the night.<|15.70|> <|18.70|> In the name of the Father and of the Son<|21.32|> <|21.32|> and of the Holy Spirit, amen.<|23.40|>
